description: Execute arbitrary JavaScript in the browser page context. Covers the string and object forms, args passthrough, plain-string and regex output assertions, serialized object return values, the outputs.result -> variables plumbing, and the unsafe-step skip path. Inherits the default firefox-headless contexts from config.json.

tests.description: Permutations that should all PASS in a real browser.

steps.description: Simple string form runs and captures a return value (no assertion).
Object form with a computed return value and a plain-string output assertion.
Arguments are passed positionally and exposed via the arguments object.
Regex output assertion against the serialized return value.
Object return values are JSON-serialized before matching.
Capture the return value into an environment variable via outputs.result.
Consume the variable set above; it is substituted into args before execution.

tests.description: An unsafe runBrowserScript step is skipped when unsafe steps aren't allowed (default).

steps.description: Marked unsafe; skipped because allowUnsafeSteps is false.
